name: Reddit r/chiangmai
note: Best for asking locals/expats where they found places, which agencies are stale, and which buildings to walk into. Threads repeatedly favour walking in and asking management over web listings.

name: ASEAN NOW forums
note: Real-estate/housing forum with 11k+ topics and an 'Advertise Your Property For Sale or Rent' thread. Good for landlord norms and older expat-owner leads.

name: Expat.com
note: Guides, community and classifieds. Worth a scan but unlikely to beat the Thai-language portals.

name: RentHub
note: Not auto-scraped: organised by apartment building (mixed room types, mostly studio/1BR) rather than per-unit 2BR listings, so it doesn't fit the 2-room filter. Useful to browse by hand if you'll consider building-managed apartments.

name: DDproperty
note: Not auto-scraped: blocks bots (HTTP 403 anti-bot challenge). 880+ CM rentals incl. 104 condos under ฿15k, but heavily agent-listed. Browse by hand.

name: FazWaz / Thaiger Property / PropertyScout
note: Large databases with good filters (incl. pet-friendly pages). Useful for market comps and seeing what exists, not for owner-direct.

name: Perfect Homes / 66 Property / Expat Homes
note: Real agencies — use as inventory/reference. Perfect Homes notes it cannot currently offer pet-friendly condos due to condo regulations.
